Home»Sport»Paris Olympics 2024»Olympics: When Akani Simbine will be in action on Sunday?

Akani Simbine will be one of the headline acts for Team SA during a busy day at the Paris Olympics on Sunday.

It’s abusy dayfor Team SA at the 2024 Paris Olympics on Sunday as several athletes strive to add to the medal tally that currently stands at four, with Akani Simbine in the running for gold.

All eyes will be on the men’s sprinters later in the day, with Simbine the medal favourite among three Team SA sprinters whoqualifiedfor the men’s 100m semi-finals, which get under way just after 20:00.

ATHLETICS

Men’s long jump qualification, Group A (11am):Jovan van Vuuren

Men’s long jump qualification, Group B (11am):Cheswill Johnson

Women’s 400m H, first round, heats (12.43pm):Rogail Joseph

Women’s 400m H, first round, heats (12.51m):Zeney Gledenhuys

Men’s 400m, round 1 heats (7.21pm):Lythe Pillay

Men’s 400m, round 1 heats (7.37pm):Zakhiti Nene

Men’s 100m, semi-finals (8.05pm):Shaun Maswanganyi

Men’s 100m, semi-finals (8.14pm):Akani Simbine

Men’s 100m, semi-finals (8.23pm):Benjamin Richardson

Women’s 800m, semi-finals (8.55pm):Prudence Sekgodiso

CYCLING

Women’s road race (2pm):Tiffany Keep, Ashleigh Moolman Pasio

GOLF

Men’s Individual, fourth round

11.55am:Christiaan Bezuidenhout

11.44am:Erik van Rooyen

ATHLETICS

Men’s 100m, first round, heats:Shaun Maswangani (10.06) finished 3rd behind Louie Hinchliffe (9.98) and Noah Lyles (10.04) to qualify for the semi-finals

Men’s 100m, first round, heats:Benjamin Richardson (10.06) finished 4th in his heat, won by Kayinsola Ajayi of Nigeria in 20.02. Richardson progressed to the semi-finals as one of the fastest from outside the top three

Men’s 100m, first round, heats:Akani Simbine (10.03) won his heat and moved through to the semi-finals

Men’s 1500m repechage:Tshepo Tshite finished 4th in his race in 3:35.45 and Ryan Mphahlele finished 11th in his heat in 3:36.64.

Neither went through to the semi-finals

CYCLING

Men’s road race:Ryan Gibbons finished 69th, nearky 20 minutes behind gold medallist Remco Eveneoel of Belgium. Evenepoel came home over a minute clear of the French duo of Valentin Madouas and Christophe Laporte.

GOLF

Men’s Individual, third round (tee-off times)

T13th 70 71 64 205Christiaan Bezuidenhout

T13th 67 69 69 205Erik van Rooyen

The leaders are Jon Rahm and Xander Schauffele on 199

HOCKEY

Women’s Pool B:Team SA 0 United States 1

ROWING

Women’s single scull, C final:Paige Badenhorst finished second to Serbia’s Jovana Arsic in 7:27.76 to claim 14th spot overall

title: Olympics: When Akani Simbine will be in action on Sunday?
author: Craig Lewis
description: Akani Simbine will be one of the headline acts for Team SA during a busy day at the Paris Olympics on Sunday.
